title: veridict multi-metric compare report
description: JSON output of `veridict compare` when multiple --metric flags are given together. A single-metric run instead produces compare-report.schema.json directly - its JSON shape never changes based on how many metrics were requested elsewhere.

schema_version.description: Stays 1 across additive changes; bumps only when a field is removed or renamed.

verdict.description: Aggregated across all reports: any fail sinks the whole run, else any inconclusive holds it back, else pass.

validity.description: Invalid if any per-metric report's own validity is invalid (a --max-timeouts/--max-crashes/--max-invalid cap breach).

promotion.description: Promoted only when validity is valid and verdict is pass. This is the deployment gate - never adjusted by --claim-correction, no matter what's passed; see simultaneous_claims_promotion for the thing correction actually adjusts.

simultaneous_claims_promotion.description: Present only when `compare --claim-correction bonferroni|holm` (or its deprecated --correction alias) was requested. Promoted only if every report's own family_adjusted_promotion is promoted too - i.e. every metric's improvement claim individually survives being read as part of a simultaneous family, distinct from promotion above (which requiring every metric to pass already makes at least as conservative as a single metric, with no correction needed for that guarantee on its own).

reports.description: One report per requested --metric, each independently computed from the same input scan.
